confirmed.note: collect_json_fields() bodies byte-identical except class name: same parent call (cpudevice::collect_json_fields) + identical JSON_KV(last_time)/JSON_FIELD(last_energy,consumed_power,device_valid). Copy was INTRODUCED here (round1 attributed the pair to the later dedup-include commit 4f8f6d19, not to its real origin).

report_gpu_stats() Frequency block duplicates the per-GT sysfs walk introduced 26 min earlier in show_frequency_section (commit 39221eb): identical for-tile/for-gt loop, identical paths freq0/{cur,min,max,rp0,rpn}_freq; only the sink differs (freq_row vector vs draw_progress_bar). partial-edited within-file copy.

false_positives.what_happened: Test stub re-implements production class methods (device ctor, register_sysfs_path, collect_json_fields) with the SAME signatures and small bodies to break the dependency chain for unit tests. A token detector sees matching signature lines + JSON_KV/JSON_FIELD idiom and flags it as a copy of the real impl, but it is a deliberate test double (different file root, tests/ tree).
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a path-class exclusion — when one side of a candidate pair lives under tests/ (or matches *stub*/*mock*/*fake* filename) and the other under src/, drop the pair. Stub-vs-impl mirroring is intentional, not drift.

false_positives.what_happened: Three collect_json_fields overrides each are long runs of JSON_FIELD(x) macro lines. The macro names repeat across overrides (same dictionary of field-serialize tokens) so token similarity is high even though the actual field SETS differ (package c-states vs core c1/c3 vs cpu). Detector can over-weight the repeated JSON_FIELD token.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: treat single-token macro-call lines (JSON_FIELD/JSON_KV style: IDENT '(' args ')' ';') as low-entropy and down-weight them in the similarity score, the same way getters/setters are; require a minimum count of non-macro distinct tokens before a block qualifies as a clone.

cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph: nodes=205 edges=392 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1. Include graph is acyclic; no closing back-edges exist.
